title: Bacterial SYBR-Cell Staining and Counting with the Attune Flow Cytometer
steps: Take foil-wrapped diluted SYBR stock out from fridge door and allow it to come to room temperature (about 5-10 minutes), while keeping it protected from light.
After warmed, vortex the SYBR stock and make sure it is well mixed

Take out samples for the day (in fridge, labeled with station, depth, date, and "BACT") and gently vortex each sample. Note: cruise 3 has BACT 1 and 2 so that we have enough volume for the imaging flow cyto as well
Clean culture hood with 70% ethanol and use UV light to sterilize the hood (about 15 minutes)
put a fresh 96-well plate, mixed samples, and mixed SYBR stock into the cleaned culture hood

Add 1 (one) µl of SYBR stock to each well you intend to use in the assay. Make sure to add the 1 µl to the bottom center of the well so that you can see it. - do not use the furthest column to the right
add 99 (ninety-nine) µl of sample to each of the other wells and mix 5-10x so that SYBR and samples are mixed. This can be printed and used to write in sample names
add 99 (ninety-nine) µl of water to well 1A - this will be a blank
put sample plate into the mini-incubator and incubate at 37ºC for 10 minutes
Record sample-well locations Either print template, use the postits, or record in protocols (makes sure you save run): Blank
Open proper experiment from template and edit sample names to match actual sample names
creator: Maggi Mars
